checks.a_analysis: The output follows the source document's text closely. No fabricated paragraphs or invented section headings were found. The added metadata and accessibility elements (titles, alt text) are expected behavior.
The document contains names of agencies (e.g., Cary Police, Chatham Co. ECC) but no specific phone numbers, emails, or URLs to check for fabrication. All names matches the source.
All pages of the source PDF are represented in the output sections. While some content within pages is omitted (captured in C5), no entire sections or pages were dropped.

All tables across the 13 pages were verified. In all cases, the row counts in the output match or slightly exceed the source (due to handling of merged cells/formatting), ensuring no data rows were lost.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There are two major instances of text omission. First, on Page 9, the descriptive text inside the flowchart boxes for 'Major NG911 Program Areas' (e.g., 'Key Consideration is 911 as a logic based...') and all labels/data within the 'Call Handling Solution Architecture' diagram are entirely missing from the semantic text elements. Second, on Page 8, the five sidebar comments ([BR1] through [BR5]) providing critical rationale for policy changes are completely omitted. These omissions represent a significant loss of information present in the original document.
checks.b_evidence_source: Major NG911 Program Areas flowchart boxes contain: 'Key Consideration is 911 as a logic based, IP enabled managed service', 'Key Considerations, no onsite PSAP personnel support is required. Full lifecycle management', etc. Solution Architecture diagram contains numerous labels like 'Allows "What If" Policy Routing Rules', '0.3 second typical call completion time', etc. Page 8 contains five comments like: 'Commented [BR2]: This sentence was modified because NC OEMS determines which programs are approved...'
checks.b_evidence_output: [The text described is absent from the corresponding sections in the JSON output, which only provides images and generic alt text for these areas.]

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: On Page 8, which is a redline/draft document, the output fails to properly interpret tracked changes. This results in the concatenation of struck-through and revised text without spacing or clarification, leading to nonsensical phrases like 'shall to' and 'or the and'.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 8 redline text: 'shall' (struck through) 'to' (added in red); IAED) or the' (struck through) 'and' (added in red).
checks.b_evidence_output: Element 53: '...Commission shall to successfully complete...'; Element 54: '...IAED) or the and NC Sheriffs' Education...'

checks.a_analysis: The output does not contain any duplicated text blocks or sections. Each page's content is rendered once in sequence, including intentional repetitions in the source like the document header on multiple pages and the two versions of the draft policy (clean and tracked changes).

The heading hierarchy is well-preserved. Major titles are assigned H1, and subsequent sections use H2 and H3 correctly without skipping levels. For example, the Minutes section uses H1 for the board name, H2 for the meeting title, and H3 for the draft status.

There is a structural defect in the table on Page 1 (Agenda). Item 5 in the table contains a bulleted sub-item. In the source, this text is clearly part of the 'Topic' column. However, the output breaks this bullet point into a completely new row (row index 7) that contains only one cell, which corrupts the 3-column grid structure of the table.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1, Agenda table, Item 5 Topic: "Training Class Eligibility • Buncombe County 911 - Priority Dispatch “EMD and EPD Refresher Course”"
checks.b_evidence_output: The table at index 2 has Row 6 with 3 cells for item 5, but Row 7 contains only a single cell: "Buncombe County 911 - Priority Dispatch “EMD and EPD Refresher Course”", breaking the column alignment.

checks.a_analysis: The linearization of the document content matches the source. Side-by-side tables on page 2 are rendered sequentially, and the logical flow from headers to agenda/minutes items is maintained.

Several structured lists in the source document have been flattened into simple paragraphs. On Page 6, the multi-level training agenda is rendered as a single block of text with newlines. On Page 13, the numbered lists for 2020 and 2021 goals are also flattened into paragraphs, losing the semantic list structure required for accessibility and structural fidelity.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 6, Buncombe County training agenda items 1-6; Page 13, 2020 and 2021 goals numbered 1-4.
checks.b_evidence_output: "1) Introductions\n2) Overview of ProQA functionality..." (Index 40); "1. Complete development of training standards..." (Index 84); "1. Develop an information sharing program..." (Index 86).

checks.a_analysis: No links were found in the source document, and the output correctly does not include any injected or fabricated links.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. The document transitions from the agenda to the meeting minutes, training outlines, infographics, and survey results in the same sequence as the source. Layout elements such as the headers, lists, and segmented sections are appropriately rendered for an HTML document.

All tables from the source PDF, including the multi-column attendee lists, voting results tables, and survey response tables, are successfully rendered as functional HTML tables. Columns are aligned, and the content is readable and logically structured. The multi-row cell in the item 6b table (Source Page 4) is handled correctly in the HTML output.

All meaningful images and logos from the source PDF are present in the HTML output. This includes the NC DIT 911 Board logo, the Priority Dispatch logo on page 6, and the complex NG9-1-1 architecture diagrams and program area graphics on page 9.

There is no evidence of body content duplication in the HTML output. Each section of text and table appears only once in the main body, corresponding to its placement in the source PDF. The inclusion of headings in the 'Contents' navigation section at the top is a standard accessibility feature and does not constitute duplication.
